title: Fit a multinomial logit model and write exports in one call
summary: Combines `fit` and `finalize` into a single command: estimates the MNL model by maximum likelihood, then immediately writes the user-facing summary and CSV exports. Emits a single started / finished event pair.
description: Reads a coded long-format choice CSV and fits an aggregate multinomial logit (MNL) model by maximum likelihood, then immediately finalises the run and writes the user-facing artifacts — `summary.json`, the coefficient table with standard errors, and any other exports — all in one protocol invocation. This is the conventional entry point when you want results without managing the fit and finalize steps separately. Equivalent to calling `fit` then `finalize` back-to-back, but with a single started / finished event pair instead of two.
usage:
[["model-mnl-lg::fit-finalize"]]
input = "<coded_long.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "lg_run"
# optional:
test_input = "<holdout.csv>"
export_dir = "<dir>"
iterations = 500
convergence_threshold = 1e-8
gradient_threshold = 1e-8
threads = 1

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the coded long-format CSV file. Expected layout: one row per (respondent, task, alternative), with a `resp_id` column, a `task` column, a response column, and one numeric column per coded covariate. This is exactly what `choice-wrangling::build-estimation-long` produces.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives the raw fit state (`spec.json`, `fit.postcard`, per-iteration metrics) and the `exports/` subdirectory with user-facing artifacts.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: test_input
arguments.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation. Must have the same column layout as `input`.

arguments.name: export_dir
arguments.description: Where to write the CSV exports. Defaults to `{run_dir}/exports`. Override if you want to keep the exports in a separate directory.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have a header row. Default `true`.

arguments.name: convergence_threshold
arguments.description: Stop when the change in log-likelihood per iteration falls below this. Default `1e-8`.

arguments.name: gradient_threshold
arguments.description: Stop when the gradient norm falls below this. Default `1e-8`.

arguments.name: rootlik_denominator
arguments.description: Denominator used when computing the root-likelihood diagnostic. Does not affect the fit itself.

arguments.name: vcov_ridge
arguments.description: Ridge added to the Hessian diagonal before inversion when computing standard errors. Default `0.0`. Raise only when the Hessian is near-singular.

details: `fit-finalize` is the single-call entry point for MNL estimation. It runs the same optimizer as `fit`, then immediately calls the same finalization logic as `finalize`, all within a single protocol event pair. The raw fit state (`spec.json`, `fit.postcard`) is persisted to `run_dir` exactly as it would be from a standalone `fit` call, so you can still run `finalize` again later if you want to write exports to a different directory.
value:
On success, `run_dir` contains the full fit state plus `exports/` with:

- `summary.json` — coefficients, log-likelihood, AIC, BIC.
- `{run_id}_coef_table.csv` — coefficient table with standard errors.

In protocol mode the plugin emits a single `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`) carrying the fit summary.
notes: Equivalent to calling `fit` then `finalize` back-to-back with the same `run_dir`.
The raw fit state is persisted, so you can re-run `finalize` separately later if needed.

see_also.description: Fit only — call `finalize` separately when needed.
Re-summarise a completed run without re-fitting.
Cross-validated fit and finalize in one call.
Produces the coded long-format CSV that this command consumes.

fields.name: input
fields.description: Path to the coded-long-format CSV file.

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: test_input
fields.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

fields.name: export_dir
fields.description: Where to write CSV exports. Defaults to {run_dir}/exports.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: convergence_threshold
fields.description: Stop when change in log-likelihood per iteration < this. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: gradient_threshold
fields.description: Stop when gradient norm < this. Default: 1e-8.

fields.name: vcov_ridge
fields.description: Ridge added to Hessian before inversion for SE computation. Default: 0.0.
